title: Fake Users and permissions for testing
excerpt: Hi, to help with learning how Monday.com works from various different angles it would be very useful to create fake user accounts which simulate actual users and the communications between them. If a system works, then t…

posts.author: EdGra (Ed)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, to help with learning how Monday.com works from various different angles it would be very useful to create fake user accounts which simulate actual users and the communications between them. If a system works, then these accounts could then be turned into real users or their permissions associated with real existing users. This is because when I test a new idea I don’t want emails / alerts etc to go to real world users but I do want to see the pathways a new systems has. Without this, it’s very hard to visualise how something will work in theory, without being able to actively see it operating. I don’t want to have to create fake email addresses for testing and cannot afford to buy user accounts for testing.
Thanks

posts.author: Steff (K)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Ed, did you find a solution?

I face the same challenge, that I do changes as an admin and want to see the visual impact to our “members”.

posts.author: Steff (K)
posts.cookedHtml:
We applied the following solution to view changes made by admins.
We created a “Monday Test” Account for Monday which our admins use to login and view any changes they made.

As we linked our login to MS365, we login with 2 different accounts in Edge Browser and can access as admin and test user at the same time.
